title: Add voronoi cellular fragment shader
overview: Create a voronoi distance-field fragment shader that renders points as faceted cellular regions (stained-glass look) instead of soft circles. Wire it into the point cloud system as an alternative fragment shader selectable via config, with audio-reactive cell boundaries and palette-compatible coloring.

steps.title: Restore all deleted working-tree files from HEAD
steps.description: The working tree has many deleted src/ and tests/ files. Restore them all in a single bulk operation so subsequent steps can focus purely on modification, not mixed restoration/editing. This prevents fragile partial-restore across multiple steps.

steps.title: Create the voronoi cellular fragment shader
steps.description: Write src/visual/shaders/voronoiCell.frag.glsl — a self-contained fragment shader that computes a 2D voronoi distance field from gl_PointCoord. The shader uses the 9-cell nearest-neighbor pattern to find cell boundaries. Bass energy stretches the cell grid (fewer, larger cells), treble energy sharpens and cracks edges. Cell interior uses vColor from the vertex shader (palette-derived RGB). Edge tinting uses simple RGB darkening with a slight cool shift — NOT an hsl2rgb round-trip — to avoid color-space mismatch since the vertex shader already outputs palette-derived RGB in vColor. Depth fog applies identically to the existing pointWarp.frag.glsl pattern.

files.details: Uniforms: uOpacity (float), uFogNear (float), uFogFar (float), uBassEnergy (float), uTrebleEnergy (float), uVoronoiGridSize (float, default ~4.0), uTime (float). Varyings: vColor (vec3), vDepth (float). Algorithm: (1) Scale gl_PointCoord by uVoronoiGridSize, modulated by bass — gridSize = uVoronoiGridSize * (1.0 - uBassEnergy * 0.3) so bass stretches cells larger. (2) For each of 9 neighbor cells, compute a pseudo-random point position using a simple hash function (fract(sin(dot())) — no noise3d import needed). (3) Find minimum distance (F1) and second-minimum distance (F2). (4) Edge detection: edgeFactor = smoothstep(edgeWidth, edgeWidth+blur, F2-F1) where edgeWidth is modulated by treble — higher treble = thinner, sharper cracks. (5) Cell color = vColor for interior. Edge color = vColor * 0.3 (darkened) with slight blue shift: vec3(vColor.r*0.25, vColor.g*0.3, vColor.b*0.45). (6) Final color = mix(edgeColor, cellColor, edgeFactor). (7) Alpha: discard if gl_PointCoord outside circle (dist>0.5), then apply soft edge, fog alpha, opacity. (8) Fog tint applied identically to pointWarp.frag.glsl.

steps.title: Add voronoi config field to QualityProfile and PointCloudConfig
steps.description: Extend QualityProfile with an enableVoronoiCells boolean (false for low tier, true for medium/high). Extend PointCloudConfig with an optional useVoronoiShader boolean. These are type/config-only changes with no runtime dependency on the shader file.

files.details: Add enableVoronoiCells: boolean to QualityProfile interface. Set to false in low tier, true in medium and high tiers.
Add useVoronoiShader?: boolean to PointCloudConfig interface.

steps.title: Wire voronoi fragment shader into the point cloud system
steps.description: Modify pointCloud.ts to conditionally import and use the voronoi fragment shader when config.useVoronoiShader is true. The vertex shader construction (noise3dGlsl + pointWarpVert) remains UNCHANGED — only the fragment shader reference is swapped. When voronoi mode is active, add the extra uniforms (uVoronoiGridSize, uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uTime) to the material. Note: uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, and uTime are already present in the uniform block; only uVoronoiGridSize is new.

files.details: At top: import voronoiFragShader from '../shaders/voronoiCell.frag.glsl?raw'. In createPointCloud: read useVoronoiShader from config (default false). In init(): select fragmentShader — if useVoronoiShader, use voronoiFragShader, otherwise use the existing pointWarp fragment. The vertex shader line (noise3dGlsl + '\n' + pointWarpVert) is NOT changed — voronoi is fragment-only. When voronoi is active, add uVoronoiGridSize uniform (initial value 4.0) to the uniforms object. In draw(): if voronoi mode, update uVoronoiGridSize based on structureComplexity (range 3.0–6.0). The existing uniform updates for uBassEnergy, uTrebleEnergy, uTime already cover what the voronoi fragment needs.

steps.title: Pass voronoi config from main.ts to createPointCloud
steps.description: In main.ts, pass the quality.enableVoronoiCells flag through to createPointCloud as useVoronoiShader. No file restoration needed — step 0 already restored all files.

files.details: In the createPointCloud call, add useVoronoiShader: quality.enableVoronoiCells to the config object.

steps.title: Write tests
steps.description: Add tests covering shader content, uniform presence, config wiring, and audio reactivity.

files.details: Tests: T-058-10: createPointCloud({ useVoronoiShader: true }) initialises without error. T-058-11: The resulting ShaderMaterial has uVoronoiGridSize uniform. T-058-12: draw() with bassEnergy=0.8 updates uVoronoiGridSize to a value reflecting structureComplexity. T-058-13: draw() with trebleEnergy=0.9 does not throw (treble is consumed in shader, no CPU-side effect to assert beyond no-crash). T-058-14: createPointCloud({ useVoronoiShader: false }) does NOT have uVoronoiGridSize uniform. T-058-15: setOpacity works on voronoi-configured point cloud. T-058-20: When useVoronoiShader is true, the ShaderMaterial's fragmentShader source contains BOTH 'uVoronoiGridSize' AND 'gl_PointCoord' AND 'F2' (voronoi second-distance variable), confirming the full voronoi shader is wired, not just any shader that declares the uniform. T-058-21: The fragment shader source does NOT contain noise3d patterns (snoise/fbm3) — confirming the voronoi shader is self-contained. T-058-22: QualityProfile for 'low' tier has enableVoronoiCells === false. T-058-23: QualityProfile for 'high' tier has enableVoronoiCells === true.

steps.title: Build verification and shader compile gate
steps.description: Run the full build to verify no TypeScript errors, no missing imports, and the GLSL files are bundled correctly via Vite's ?raw imports.

testStrategy: Unit tests verify shader selection logic, uniform presence/absence based on config, quality tier propagation, and shader source content (multiple voronoi-specific patterns). Integration is covered by the build step which validates TypeScript types, import resolution, and Vite raw GLSL bundling. Manual verification: load the app with medium/high quality to confirm voronoi cells render as faceted stained-glass regions with audio-reactive edges, and that low quality falls back to the soft-circle shader.

acceptanceCriteriaMapping.A new fragment shader renders points/geo: Steps 1, 3 — voronoiCell.frag.glsl computes voronoi distance field from gl_PointCoord, wired as alternative fragment in pointCloud.ts
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Cell boundaries shift with audio energy: Step 1 — bass modulates gridSize (fewer larger cells), treble modulates edgeWidth (sharper cracks)
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Color mapping respects the existing pale: Step 1 — cell interior uses vColor (palette-derived RGB from vertex shader), edge tint is RGB darkening of vColor
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Shader compiles cleanly with no WebGL er: Step 6 — build gate + existing ShaderErrorCollector catches compile errors at runtime
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Can be assigned to at least one existing: Steps 2–4 — useVoronoiShader config on PointCloudConfig, driven by QualityProfile.enableVoronoiCells
acceptanceCriteriaMapping.Depth fog still applies: Step 1 — fog computation identical to pointWarp.frag.glsl using uFogNear, uFogFar, vDepth
